However, I had to be my own advocate, providing the documentation and hardship letters that eventually increased my payout my thousands of dollars.. steps that Brad himself told me were "meaningless” because the policy limit was set and it “wasn’t even a lot of money”. His words!   However it was only “meaningless” and “not a lot of money” for them. Every dollar I get back from the medical liens, is a dollar towards my surgeries and recovery. I even told him this because I just couldn’t believe that he would say that to me, knowing I was without teeth and struggling to pay for my recovery. It was almost as if he wanted me to pay them 33% of the money for pretty much no reason. He hated that I was actually asking them to do their jobs. It’s actually kind of sad. I am a single woman of color, without any family nearby and it did seem that they took advantage of my situation if I’m being 100% honest. I mean the fact that I was hit by a car 03/25/2025 and was paid out before the year was even over says a lot. It was an okay payout but really just barely enough to stabilized and get the surgeries I needed. I believe I could have gotten more if they actually wanted to fight for me.

The office also lacks professionalism. I was sent incorrect emails, treated rudely by staff, and was handed my final check without a simple ID check… a major security red flag. I simply said I was there for a check and the woman handed it right over. No care for my case whatsoever. While Brad eventually took over my case and negotiated a better outcome, I had to press him to do so, and his demeanor was often dismissive of my lived reality as a pedestrian in a major accident. reviews.text:
I do not ever write reviews but I strongly feel like I need to share my experience with this firm to warn others. I retained Family Law Matters in March 2020 for a Domestic Violence Restraining Order including custody (This was right before the COVID pandemic struck and the courts shut down). I paid a one-time retention fee and agreed to a monthly membership fee, which I was told included unlimited attorney/ paralegal access with phone calls/ emails. My hearing date was set for 3/27 and I anticipated all-in-all I'd be paying around $4,200.00.

While Family Law Matters claims to be understanding of the economic hardships COVID has impacted on people, they will gladly take every penny they can get from you without any negotiation. With that being said, my court date ended up getting extended 4 times due to the COVID court closures (3/27, 4/30, 7/7 & 8/4). I ended up having to pay an additional $4,200.00 just in monthly membership fees, at no fault of my own, due to the court closures and they refused to negotiate any type of relief option. They tried justifying this by sending me an itemized statement showing how much I would've owed had I been charged hourly. This totaled nearly $10,000.00 (mainly phone calls and emails, which was covered under my monthly membership fee!).

I strongly feel they used COVID to their advantage and continued charging these fees while waiting for the courts to reopen. This is unethical and immoral - Especially for a firm that boasts about their Christian beliefs.

Additionally, my paralegal, Charlene, was always extremely rude and always seemed very bothered every time I would reach out. Yes, I certainly had a lot of questions as I had just left a very abusive relationship with my child's father. I relied heavily on their professional opinion and I was made to feel like a burden.

Finally, I was able to complete my hearing on 8/4 and I was told a judgment would need to be filed with the courts and we'd be done! Well come 9/4/20, I get an email receipt for another monthly installment! The judgment was not filed with the court until 8/24 and now I have the option to either file a substitution of attorney, self-represent and hope and pray the judgment was filed correctly OR continue to pay these monthly installments until the court finalizes it.

All in all, I am extremely disappointed with this firm's moral and ethical standards, especially Beshoy who tried to justify it.

Unfortunately, due to the COVID-19 pandemic, court delays are out of our control. However, we will continue to monitor your case closely to ensure your judgment is entered and your case is completed. We simply ask for your patience as the court navigates these uncharted times. We hope to deliver good news to you shortly. Please feel free to call our office and discuss any further concerns or questions that you may have. We wish you the best of luck in all of your future endeavors. God Bless.

reviews.text: I was wrongfully terminated from my job on November 16th, 2024. I had been extremely ill and had to go to urgent care on Wednesday, November 6th. My visit at the urgent care was brief because I was then advised to immediately go the emergency room at the hospital. My brother drove me to urgent care and then to the hospital. We spent 7 hours in the emergency room from 9pm to 3am. I was diagnosed with gastroenteritis. Finally, when I left the emergency room, the doctor gave me a note indicating that I should return to work on Monday, November 11th. My job allowed me to work the entire week and then fired me at the end that week on a Saturday at the closing of my shift. She told me that this was notice of my termination due to their policy associated with sick time. I replied that I had dropped off a doctor's note indicating that I was excused from work because of my illness. She replied by saying that we don't accept doctor's notes. I then filled out the paperwork for my termination. I then reached out to my attorney Aashish Bhargava. I explained the details of my termination to him and he said he believed that due to my specific circumstances, I was wrongfully terminated. The dedication and urgency that Aashish put into my case was unparalleled. He fought arduously for 3 months and came out victorious which awarded me with a payment that was three times what I had imagined getting. If you're looking for an attorney that wastes no time and meticulously handles each step of the process, Aashish Bhargava exudes perseverance. I would recommend him to anyone that was terminated. He definitely has a passion for helping others.

I retained an attorney service/firm to represent me in my divorce and family law matters, but my experience was extremely disappointing. Despite spending nearly $30,000 on their services, I feel like I received nothing in return. While they did go to court, they never even served my ex-wife properly, which ultimately bought her six more weeks in the house she was no longer paying for. Instead of taking meaningful action, they simply requested continuances, prolonging the process without any real progress.

They also failed to advocate for me in critical matters. They never took action against my ex-wife for denying me the court-ordered time with my children, and they never helped me gain access to my home to retrieve my belongings. In the end, I had to go through a criminal court judge just to collect my personal property—something my family law attorneys should have handled.

Ultimately, my ex-wife, who was the primary provider, was awarded custody of the children and retained everything, while I was left with nothing. I deeply regret trusting this firm, as I do not believe they truly fought for my rights or best interests

Do yourself a favor and stay far away. Expensive as heck, having 3-4 different people touching your file and running up your bill. But they managed to turn my 18 month alimony arrangement to 21… RUN! $6,000 mistake!

Please read this in entirety
My name is Stephanie. I worked for a lab for 22 years. My job was to manage and perform logistics for blood collection. One day while I was picking up specimens I stepped on a rock which caused me to fall and sustain a sprain to my left ankle and a right knee meniscus tear. I reported my injury as you are supposed to. My employer did not send me to a doctor until Monday - two days after my injury - and I was in excruciating pain. This is where my nightmare started. They sent me to a place called Concentra. I waited for approx 2 1/2 hours to be seen. I received an x-ray which showed no break. I was immediately given a physical therapy appointment on that Monday with a second appointment on Tuesday. The discomfort was very bad. My next appointment I waited for 3 hours before I was seen for my follow up. After the follow up I was scheduled for additional PT. Was given exercises that only increased the discomfort which I reported to the therapist at the time. I had an additional appointment which I had another 3 hour wait. I was placed in an area with a curtain between me and another patient and waited another hour. I did not see any attending physicians during my time of treatment except for with a specialist which was for approximately 5 minutes. It took me 1 1/2 years to get a simple meniscus tear surgery.

i would like to say work comp is nothing but running around and complete red tape. There is no concern for the patient. Mty attorney can jump up and down but he can't do much to get medical help because his hands are tied. The insurance company controls the doctors and treatment. I would like to thank RP Law Group along with Denise Johnson and all the ladies at RP Law Group are a pleasure to deal with while pushing for my case.
I recomend RP Law
